module_dispositions.plain_language_proposition: A finite request-to-closure lifecycle keeps resource identity, units, direct and displaced cost, verification cost, uncertainty, protected floors, capacity, queue policy, observed spend, useful outcome, simulation fidelity, residual, and reconciliation records joined while rejecting raw-proxy promotion and authority leakage.
module_dispositions.modeled_state: Nine authored stages and 66 routes over request, policy, rights, resource, evaluator, simulation, result, capacity, queue, verification, transfer, reconciliation, and closure fields, with twelve bounded result artifacts digest-bound by an independent consumer.
assumptions: All resource inventories, units, costs, capacity limits, queue policies, useful outcomes, verifier results, simulation declarations, residuals, descendants, and digests are truthful and complete for the declared boundary.
The lifecycle does not allocate real capacity, measure opportunity cost, validate a simulator, observe a natural task, or enforce a queue in a deployed system.
A closed resource record does not establish that the chosen allocation was efficient, optimal, fair, useful, safe, or preferable to a simpler allocation policy.
countermodels: Missing protected floors, reviewer or verifier capacity, failure retention, or a useful-outcome record blocks the corresponding stage.
A raw throughput, memory, cost, or compression proxy cannot promote support without independent verification and an evidence transition.
A simulated claim above its declared fidelity, or without scope, temporal semantics, resource bill, omissions, and transfer decision, cannot reconcile.
module_dispositions.mutation_evidence: The independent consumer exercises 66 routes, rejects 57 of 57 mutations, and digest-binds twelve bounded resource and simulation results while preserving support and external effects at none.
module_dispositions.maximum_inference: Adequate for exact finite allocation-record ordering, identity, protected-floor, proxy-blocking, simulation-fidelity, reconciliation, and closure consequences. It does not establish inventory completeness, measured efficiency, optimal allocation, useful throughput, fairness, simulator validity, transfer, safety, deployment, or support movement.

module_dispositions.plain_language_proposition: A finite artifact lifecycle preserves exact artifact and lineage identity from registration through provenance, replay, observed cross-check, bounded trust-base review, revocation closure, consumer acknowledgment, and admission without assigning support or external effects.
module_dispositions.modeled_state: Seven authored states with artifact, parent-job, source, context, transaction, certificate, tool, claim, test, policy, consumer, replay, observation, verifier, trust-root, recursion-stop, residual, revocation, and receipt records across eight exact independent suites.
assumptions: Every artifact identity, digest, lineage reference, certificate, replay grade, observation, cross-check, trust root, verifier separation, revocation, residual, and consumer acknowledgment is authentic and complete within the declared trusted boundary.

The finite state machine does not establish open-world provenance completeness, physical actuality, semantic equivalence, hardware trust, adversarial robustness, or the competence of the independent verifier.
Admission is a record transition for one named consumer and does not establish truth, durability, ownership, utility, release readiness, or deployment authority.
countermodels: Missing parent job, transaction, certificate, audit trail, replay metadata, active certificate, or replay validation blocks progress.
A missing observed artifact, independent cross-check, trap challenge, or attestation limit blocks the reality cross-check.
Self-verification, a missing recursion stop or outside-TCB residual, incomplete revocation, identity substitution, replay, or authority request prevents admission.
module_dispositions.mutation_evidence: The independent consumer exercises 33 routes, rejects 53 mutations, and preserves eight exact replay, reality, receipt, attestation, and trust-base suites without support movement.
module_dispositions.maximum_inference: Adequate for exact finite artifact/lineage custody, stage ordering, receipt, replay-gate, observation-gate, bounded trust-base, revocation, and no-authority consequences. It does not establish source truth, open-world completeness, semantic or physical reality, verifier competence, durable provenance, ownership, usefulness, release, deployment, or support movement.

module_dispositions.plain_language_proposition: Finite steward decisions require scoped work contracts, protected-action approval, release records, sunset review, separated contribution ledgers, and bounded federation contracts before their authored routes permit ordinary work, publication, ledger acceptance, or dispatch.
module_dispositions.modeled_state: Hand-authored Boolean reviews for work contracts, protected actions, release gates, sunset, lifecycle routing, contribution ledgers, and federation dispatch, plus a two-valid/six-invalid independent lifecycle probe.
assumptions: Objectives, authority, tool, budget, approval, evidence, contribution, conflict, data-class, and policy fields are truthful and complete for the reviewed action.
The model does not authenticate approvals, execute tools, spend funds, allocate compensation, measure reputation, resolve conflicts, publish a release, or enforce a charter.
A dispatched contract or accepted ledger does not establish agent competence, good stewardship, ownership, fair compensation, effective governance, safe autonomy, or successful work.
countermodels: A protected action without explicit approval cannot execute, and an autonomy increase or over-policy spend routes to approval.
A tainted unreviewed event routes to quarantine and satisfied sunset criteria without an opened review blocks ordinary work.

Collapsed contribution scoring, missing credit dimensions, inherited project authority, absent work contracts, disallowed data, or missing evidence bundles prevent ledger acceptance or federation dispatch.
module_dispositions.mutation_evidence: The independent lifecycle probe preserves two valid routes and rejects six expected-invalid controls without executing steward work or changing support; the theorem surface separately covers all seven public contracts.
module_dispositions.maximum_inference: Adequate for the exact finite implications and route precedence encoded by the seven steward review types. It does not establish field authenticity, agent competence, good stewardship, ownership, fair governance, effective approval, real execution, release validity, federation safety, or support movement.

module_dispositions.plain_language_proposition: A finite artifact-to-consumption lifecycle keeps full-source, codec, decoder, platform, reconstruction, integrity, consumer-probe, fallback, qualification, observed-outcome, residual, descendant, and closure records joined while blocking raw-ratio promotion and unqualified use.
module_dispositions.modeled_state: Eight authored stages and 53 routes over exact artifact, consumer, use, policy, rights, codec, decoder, evidence, and result identities, with full-artifact fallback and bounded fixture/metadata decisions.
assumptions: Artifact, codec, decoder, platform, byte, residual, integrity, probe, rare-case, rights, fallback, cost, observed-outcome, descendant, and digest records are truthful and complete for the named use.
The route model does not compress an artifact, prove decoder determinism across platforms, measure semantic preservation, establish rare-case coverage, or execute a real consumer fallback.
A qualified finite route and favorable byte ratio do not establish useful compression, efficiency, general fidelity, durability, secure decoding, or superiority to storing the original artifact.
countermodels: A failed consumer probe routes to the full artifact when available and blocks when the fallback artifact is absent.
Exact-replay requirements without readiness, missing integrity or adversarial mutation, or missing reconstruction evidence block progression.
Unqualified use, raw-ratio promotion, missing evidence transition, missing observed outcome, residual, descendants, result digest, or cleanup blocks consumption or closure.
module_dispositions.mutation_evidence: The independent consumer exercises 53 routes and rejects 44 of 44 mutations while digest-binding the exact fixture, RAW0 replay, NEURAL0 metadata, and two bounded no-change decisions without support or external effects.
module_dispositions.maximum_inference: Adequate for exact finite compression-record identity, route ordering, reconstruction/probe/fallback gates, raw-ratio blocking, observed closure, and no-authority consequences. It does not establish semantic preservation, decoder portability, useful compression, efficiency, rare-case coverage, durability, security, deployment, or support movement.

semantic_separations.cost_and_efficiency: A complete resource bill or lower observed cost is not economic efficiency, optimal allocation, useful throughput, or a favorable joint frontier.
semantic_separations.artifact_and_reality: A digest-bound artifact with replay and cross-check receipts is not open-world truth, physical actuality, semantic equivalence, or complete provenance.
semantic_separations.stewardship_and_ownership: A valid steward contract, approval record, contribution ledger, or federation route is not competent stewardship, legitimate governance, fair compensation, or ownership.
semantic_separations.compression_and_usefulness: A byte ratio, reconstruction record, or qualified finite route is not useful compression, portable decoding, semantic preservation, durability, or superiority to the full artifact.
semantic_separations.closure_and_lifecycle_success: A closed finite record means only that declared fields passed an authored route; it is not evidence that the real lifecycle was complete, effects were controlled, or the consumer succeeded.
semantic_separations.record_authority: No resource, artifact, steward, or compression record assigns chapter support, release, publication, ownership, or external-effect authority by itself.

cluster_maximum_inference: The cluster is adequate for exact bounded resource, artifact, steward, and compression record semantics, including route ordering, identity custody, rejection, fallback, reconciliation, and no-authority consequences. It proves no inventory complete, no cost efficient or optimal, no artifact true or durable, no steward competent or legitimate, no ownership established, no compression useful or semantically faithful, no lifecycle successful, and no empirical, transfer, deployment, SOTA, AGI, ASI, support, release, or publication claim.
